description: One-pass review of crate secretx-wolfhsm (secretx-wolfhsm/). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Pass 1 (my-oss correctness) starting. Reading wolfhsm crate source at ~/.cargo/registry/.../wolfhsm-0.1.0 to verify error taxonomy and API contracts.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two real correctness bugs (transient->permanent error mapping, DataLost flattening), three real maintainer-burden risks (URI grammar doc, broken-connection persistence, undocumented size limit). No security or data-integrity issues beyond the documented non-atomic put. No unsafe code in the crate itself.
Correctness confidence: 80% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (a) whether the dead-connection-never-resets path actually fires for typical wolfhsm transport errors — would need a live HSM with intentional disconnect to verify which Error variants come back; (b) whether wolfhsm::Client::nvm_overwrite is truly the only call that returns DataLost or whether other paths (nvm_add on full quota?) also do. Uncertainty driven by: no integration test environment available; relied on reading wolfhsm-0.1.0 source to verify error contracts.
Pass 1 (my-oss correctness) complete. Findings: 2 P0, 4 P1, 2 P2, 1 P3, 2 P4, 3 decision-records. Starting pass 2 (rust-idiom).
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code reads as written by someone fluent in Rust; the only real idiom complaints are the runtime typestate (connected_client expect) and the empty-string sentinel for server. Everything else is taste. No unsafe, no leaky lifetimes, no macro abuse, no .clone() to escape borrowck, error variants are structured.
Rust idiom confidence: 90% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: none — the surface is small (~775 lines, single file, no traits beyond what core defines). Uncertainty driven by: did not run cargo clippy or cargo build (review-only mode) so machine-flagged lints unchecked. Would raise confidence by running clippy at -W clippy::pedantic.
title: Review: secretx-wolfhsm rusty pass
